title: Senecio calophyllus
taxonomy: Senecio calophyllus is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that also includes daisies and sunflowers. Within the family Asteraceae, Senecio calophyllus is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants commonly known as ragworts or groundels. This tribe is further divided into several subtribes, with Senecio calophyllus belonging to the subtribe Senecioninae. The species itself is likely classified within the genus Senecio, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as ragworts.

intro: Senecio calophyllus, also known as Woolly Groundsel, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and respiratory issues such as asthma. The bioactive constituents of Senecio calophyllus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In herbal medicine, Senecio calophyllus is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health and relieve respiratory discomfort.
